steps.title: Use the scale shortcut: find the linear ratio
steps.content:
Since $\overline{LK} \parallel \overline{RT}$, triangle $SKL \sim$ triangle $STR$ (AA: shared angle $S$, and $\angle LKS = \angle RTS = 90°$). The linear scale factor is:
$$\frac{LK}{RT} = \frac{24}{72} = \frac{1}{3}$$

steps.title: Recover ST from the area
steps.content:
Area of right triangle $RST = \frac{1}{2} \cdot RT \cdot ST$:
$$468 = \frac{1}{2} \cdot 72 \cdot ST \implies ST = 13$$

steps.title: Apply scale factor to find SK, then KT
steps.content:
Corresponding sides scale by $\frac{1}{3}$, so $SK = \frac{1}{3} \cdot ST = \frac{1}{3} \cdot 13 = \frac{13}{3}$.
$$KT = ST - SK = 13 - \frac{13}{3} = \frac{39 - 13}{3} = \frac{26}{3} \approx 8.667$$
The answer is 26/3, 8.666, or 8.667.
